mnemonics: LIT: Lists are Indexed, Tuples are Immutable. Use this to remember the key difference between mutable lists and immutable tuples.
keyPoints: Lists are mutable, ordered collections that can be changed after creation
Tuples are immutable, ordered collections that cannot be modified after creation
Dictionaries are unordered collections of key-value pairs that provide fast lookups
commonMistakes: Attempting to modify a tuple after creation (which raises an error)
Using a mutable object like a list as a dictionary key (which is not allowed)
Assuming dictionary order is preserved (in versions before Python 3.7, dicts were unordered)
